titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward Mycue Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff, Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 11 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday May 27, 2026 at 23:04
Updated by Tina Evans to describe May 28 2025 accession.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Edward Mycue Papers, which date from 1917 to 2025, contain extensive correspondence, writings, artwork, working files, notes, clippings, audiovisual and born digital materials, along with other documentation related to his life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.
Boxes 114-116, 135 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 117-118, 134 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bolerium Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2011.
August 2015 acquisition: Gift of Edward Mycue, 2015.
January 2017 acquisition: Purchased from Andover Street Archives on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.
May 2025 acquisition: Gift of Edward Mycue, 2025.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: August 2011 acquisition, August 2015 acquisition, January 2017 acquisition, and May 2025 acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Edward Mycue (1937- ) is an American poet born in Niagara Falls, New York. Mycue was a MacDowell Colony Fellow in 1974 and has published over a dozen volumes of poetry. He is editor of Norton Coker Press. Mycue was also a Peace Corps teacher in Ghana. His partner, Richard Steger, has illustrated several of Mycue's books, including Nightboats and Damage Within the Community .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward Mycue Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Edward Mycue Papers, which date from 1917 to 2025, contain extensive correspondence, writings, artwork, working files, notes, clippings, audiovisual and born digital materials, along with other documentation related to his life and work. Included are materials concerning his work as editor at Norton Coker Press and as a volunteer with the Peace Corps in Ghana.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Edward Mycue Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Writings, business and personal papers, and artwork

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, drafts, notes, correspondence, working and project files, printed material, artwork, and other documentation related to Edward Mycue's life and work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes inscribed Brian Monte manuscript, family correspondence, and rejection letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with James Broughton and various academics.

did.unittitle: Correspondence with Carol Schneck and Charlie Nash
Correspondence with Carol Schneck and Charlie Nash
Correspondence with Carol Schneck, Charlie Nash, Laura Kennelly (poet and sports journalist)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of writings by Mycue including Damagers, Assault on Summer, and Muddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of writings by Mycue including Damagers, Assault on Summer, and Muddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of writings by Mycue including Damagers, Assault on Summer, and Muddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publishing files and correspondence with DJ Powell, Will Inman, and Simon Perchik.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lawrence Fixel, Laura Kennelly, Jack Gilbert, John Gill, and Bruce McPherson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lawrence Fixel, Laura Kennelly, Jack Gilbert, John Gill, and Bruce McPherson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lawrence Fixel, Laura Kennelly, Jack Gilbert, John Gill, and Bruce McPherson.

did.unittitle: Correspondence including acceptances and letters from editors
Correspondence including acceptances and letters from editors

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Includes publications Mycue worked on Pajandrum Books, published by Dennis Koran.

did.unittitle: Magazines with Mycue contributions, papers on local literary life, and correspondence
Large pieces, posters, and artwork by Richard Steger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes some correspondence.

did.unittitle: Posters for readings and other events
Posters for readings and other events
Posters for readings and other events
Posters for readings and other events
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Norton Coker and Mother's Hen press manuscripts with related correspondence
Typed and photocopy versions of poems, drafts, and rejections
Typed and photocopy versions of poems, drafts, and rejections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Will Inman, Laura Riding Jackson, Ann Stanford, Meanjin, No Apologies, and others.

did.unittitle: Poems, small press books, and alternative publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Edward Field and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Edward Field and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with George Oppen and James Merrill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with George Oppen and James Merrill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings from members of a poetry group run by Mycue. Includes photocopies of numerous poems from 1970-2000, some unpublished, by authors including Nanos Valoritis and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mostly poetry correspondence, rejections, calendar-journals, personal papers, notes, and other documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to James Broughton, Cid Corman, Jack Foley, Dale Blair, David Gascoyne and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to James Broughton, Cid Corman, Jack Foley, Dale Blair, David Gascoyne and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to Lawrence Fixel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes papers related to Norton Coker and the Took magazine project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published versions of Mycue poems from magazines and other publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published versions of Mycue poems from magazines and other publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published versions of Mycue poems from magazines and other publications.

Submission logs that note acceptances and rejections

Correspondence, acceptances and rejections, and chain art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "X-Ray of Photo of Ed Mycue" by Richard Steger, "Damage" by Richard Steger, print on canvas, and "Clearing Winter Storm, Yosemite National Park, Ca., 1944" by E. H. Williams, Dec. 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes cover art for Gypsy Table and collage by B. Newman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters for "Munster Poster," "Bibliography 3," and "Analogical Aesthetics 6."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two unidentified portraits.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition consists of computer printouts of writings by Edward Mycue, including "Necessary Conditions," and photograph of Mycue and Terrie Schweitzer from Flickr account.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Andover Street Archives on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Writings and Professional Papers, 1960-2016. II. Correspondence, circa 1926-2016. III. Personal Papers, 1917-2016. IV. Printed Material, 1968-2016. V. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1998-2013, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition includes poems by Edward Mycue, materials related to his work in the Peace Corps, and other professional papers, correspondence, personal papers, printed material, and computer and audiovisual media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries primarily contains computer printout drafts of poems by Mycue. Also included are Peace Corps materials, resumes, papers related to his position at the Grace Cathedral book shop, and other materials documenting Mycue's career. Some files contain publishing correspondence, printed material, and handwritten notes by Mycue, as well as writings by other authors.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Poems & correspondence drafts etc. collections for possible publ. / & corres. abt reading in public places [1 of 7]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some works by other poets.

Poem collections gathered in preparation / Archives poem collections [1 of 11]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, notes, publishing correspondence, and printed material. Includes filed related to poetry readings by Mycue, Legends, children's poems, and poems for a television taping.

Rades, Jane. "On the Outer Edge of Life" [chapbook with pinback button attached, thank you note to Mycue]
"Hive of Voices: New Babels Grow," exhibition text and label on poster board

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains correspondence with publishers and editors, colleagues, family, and friends of Mycue. Correspondents include Paul Green from Spectacular Diseases Press, author Inger Fredriksson and visual artist Christer Themptander, and Mycue's partner Richard Stenger. Also included is artwork sent to Mycue by family members and printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems by Mycue, ephemera related to Bono's wedding, clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes address books, notes, lists, travel documents, tax records, receipts, tickets, brochures, clippings, artwork, photographs, and family histories.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Various materials related to travel, cultural activities [1 of 5]
Various materials related to travel, cultural activities [2 of 5]
Various materials related to travel, cultural activities [3 of 5]
Various materials related to travel, cultural activities [4 of 5]
Various materials related to travel, cultural activities [5 of 5]
Addresses, notes, & stuff from computer area
"Poetica ... the angel of poetry," multimedia artwork by Geri Digiorno, director of Petaluma Poetry Walk
Various [Mycue's Authors Guild card, ephemera, computer printouts]
Photograph of Mycue by Roslynn Koran [poster print]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains posters, fliers, programs, directories, menus, tickets, and newspaper clippings. Also included are signed poetry broadsides by Mycue and other authors.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Poetry broadsides by other authors, inscribed to Mycue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of Mycue's hard drive, compact discs, and audiocassettes.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "Poems by Bert Meyers & Odette Meyers read by Odette - April 1998"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this acquisition arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The acquisition contains correspondence, writings, printed material and other papers related to Edward Mycue.

did.unittitle: I am a fact not a fiction selected poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains two copies, one copy signed and with manuscript notes laid in.

did.unittitle: I am a fact not a fiction selected poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains two copies of the second edition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains unsorted papers, as found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a review of Song of San Francisco by Edward Mycue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains award for I am a fact not a fiction selected poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The files contain unsorted poems, as found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains correspondence and other papers relating to Jo-Anne Rosen.

did.unittitle: Tarwater, Alison: Reopening a secondary year of pandemic poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a signed and inscribed edition.
